sections.paragraph:
μακρόθυμος, ον (s. two prec. entries and next; M. Ant. 6, 30, 10; Anth. Pal. 11, 317, 1; LXX; TestJob 21:4; TestDan 6:9; JosAs 11 cod. A [p. 54, 7 Bat.] and Pal 364) pert. to being self-controlled in the face of provocation, patient, forbearing, tolerant, even-tempered.

ⓐ of human beings (Pr 14:29; 15:18; 16:32 al.) Hv 1, 2, 3; m 5, 1, 1; cp. 2; D 3:8. μακρόθυμον εἶναι Hm 8:10.

ⓑ of God (PLond VI 1917, 25 [IV a.d., Christ.]; Ex 34:6; Num 14:18; 2 Esdr 19:17 al.) Hs 8, 11, 1. W. φιλάνθρωπος Dg 8:7; ὁ μ. the one who is patient i.e. God 3:6. τὸ μ. αὐτοῦ βούλημα 1 Cl 19:3.—TW.

prec. prec. = preceding

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

TestDan TestDan = Testament of Dan, s. Test12Patr—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Hv Hv = Visions

m m = Mandates

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Christ. Christ. = Christian

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 613
